skill.name: pytdc
skill.description: Use PyTDC resources for Therapeutics Data Commons datasets, benchmark groups, scaffold and cold splits, molecular oracles, and therapeutic ML workflows.
skill.summary: Access PyTDC drug discovery datasets, benchmark templates, split strategies, metrics, and molecular oracle workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high and critical alerts, but review found they are false positives from Markdown backticks, scientific target names, molecular property acronyms, and documentation URLs. The remaining publishable risk is low because the skill includes runnable PyTDC example scripts and expected external dataset or documentation access, with no evidence of credential handling, shell execution, obfuscation, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Runnable example scripts load external scientific datasets
low_findings.description: The skill bundles Python scripts for PyTDC workflows. They do not invoke shells or handle secrets, but running them may trigger PyTDC dataset access and local caching through the PyTDC library.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are executable Python examples and import PyTDC dataset or oracle classes. The observed behavior is legitimate scientific data access, not command execution or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation includes external project links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to TDC documentation, benchmark pages, and related project resources. These are expected reference links, but users should know the skill directs them to external sites.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references to tdcommons.ai, readthedocs.io, and GitHub. I found no evidence that secrets or local data are sent to these locations.

low_findings.title: Static high-severity terms are scientific false positives
low_findings.description: Terms flagged as C2 keywords, weak cryptography, or private-key access are biological targets, molecular metrics, dictionary keys, and seed variables used in PyTDC examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows DRD2, GSK3B, QED, SA, and keys are PyTDC domain concepts or normal Python operations. No cryptographic implementation, C2 behavior, or certificate material is present.

content.value_statement: Drug discovery teams need consistent datasets, splits, and metrics for therapeutic ML work. This skill provides PyTDC guidance, examples, and templates for datasets, benchmarks, and molecular oracles.
actual_capabilities: Explains PyTDC dataset categories for ADME, toxicity, DTI, DDI, and molecular generation.
Shows how to load datasets and create scaffold, random, cold drug, and cold target splits.
Provides benchmark evaluation templates with five-seed reporting and standard metrics.
Documents molecular oracle usage for QED, SA, LogP, DRD2, GSK3B, and related targets.
Includes example scripts for loading data, running benchmark evaluations, and testing generation workflows.
Lists reference datasets, utilities, and external documentation links for deeper PyTDC usage.
limitations: It does not include trained predictive models or validated drug candidates.
PyTDC dataset loading may require internet access and local storage for downloaded data.
Molecular oracle scores are computational estimates and not experimental validation.
Users must supply their own model code for training, prediction, and deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare ADME and toxicity data
use_cases.description: Load curated molecular property datasets and choose scaffold splits for realistic lead optimization studies.

use_cases.title: Benchmark therapeutic models
use_cases.description: Compare model predictions across TDC benchmark groups with consistent metrics and multi-seed reporting.

use_cases.title: Evaluate generated molecules
use_cases.description: Score candidate SMILES strings with molecular oracles for drug-likeness, accessibility, and target activity.

prompt_templates.title: Load a dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to load the Caco2_Wang ADME dataset with PyTDC and inspect the train, valid, and test splits.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a split strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between random, scaffold, cold drug, and cold target splits for a drug-target interaction task.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate model predictions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PyTDC evaluation workflow for my ADMET model using five seeds, MAE, RMSE, and mean plus standard deviation reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Design an oracle workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a goal-directed molecular generation loop that scores candidate SMILES with QED, SA, LogP, and GSK3B oracles.

output_examples.input: Load the hERG toxicity dataset and explain the split options.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the toxicity task, recommends a scaffold split for molecular generalization, and summarizes train, validation, and test usage.

output_examples.input: Compare random and cold target splits for BindingDB_Kd.
output_examples.output: The response explains that random splits measure interpolation, while cold target splits test generalization to unseen proteins.

output_examples.input: Score generated molecules for drug-likeness and target activity.
output_examples.output: The response outlines oracle selection, batch scoring, ranking, and cautions that oracle scores need experimental follow-up.

best_practices: Use scaffold or cold splits when you need realistic generalization estimates.
Report benchmark results across all required seeds with mean and standard deviation.
Cache datasets locally and record PyTDC versions for reproducible experiments.
anti_patterns: Do not treat random split scores as proof of prospective drug discovery performance.
Do not present oracle scores as experimental evidence of efficacy or safety.
Do not mix benchmark test data into model selection or hyperparameter tuning.

faq.question: What is PyTDC?
faq.answer: PyTDC is the Python interface for Therapeutics Data Commons, a collection of drug discovery datasets and benchmarks.

faq.question: Does this skill train models?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and templates. Users provide model code and training infrastructure.

faq.question: Which datasets does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers ADME, toxicity, high-throughput screening, DTI, DDI, molecular generation, retrosynthesis, and related therapeutic tasks.

faq.question: Why are scaffold splits important?
faq.answer: Scaffold splits place structurally distinct molecules in the test set, which better reflects new chemical series.

faq.question: Can it work offline?
faq.answer: Documentation works offline, but PyTDC dataset loading may need internet access unless data is already cached.

faq.question: Are molecular oracle scores final evidence?
faq.answer: No. Oracle scores are computational estimates that require expert review and experimental validation.
